73637.subject: 🔷 The Daily: UK sanctions HTX
73637.body:
Robinhood clears final regulatory approval for WonderFi deal | Strive leapfrogs Coinbase, Riot with latest $85.4 million bitcoin buy | Hyperliquid rolls out validator-governed prediction markets for real-world events | Nathan Allman, founder and CEO of Ondo Finance, dies unexpectedly |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Daniel Kuhn

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Tuesday! *Bitcoin's* latest wobble below $77,000 reflects a market growing increasingly sensitive to Iran-related geopolitical headlines, according to analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402552/optimism-looks-fragile-bitcoin-wavers-as-iran-strikes-revive-geopolitical-tensions-and-analysts-warn-of-range-trap?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) , with rising leverage and underwater buyers risking a prolonged $72,000-$82,000 range unless fresh institutional demand returns.

In today's newsletter, the *UK* sanctions *HTX* over support for *Russia* , *Robinhood* clears the final regulatory approval for its acquisition of *WonderFi* , *Ondo Finance's* CEO dies unexpectedly, and more.

Meanwhile, *TeraWulf* stock jumps ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402616/terawulf-stock-jumps-12-news-kentucky-data-center-site-acquisition?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) over 12% on news of its Kentucky data center site acquisition.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🇬🇧 UK sanctions HTX over support of Russia in broad sweep over crypto exchanges*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/402591/uk-sanctions-htx-over-support-of-russia-in-broad-sweep-over-crypto-exchanges?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The *UK* sanctioned crypto exchange *HTX* , formerly known as *Huobi* , accusing the platform of supporting the *Russian government* through financial services linked to sanctions evasion.

* British authorities specifically alleged HTX provided financial services or economic resources to Russian-linked firms, including *A7 LLC* and *Garantex Europe OU*.
* The sanctions form part of a broader UK crackdown targeting crypto exchanges, banks, and companies accused of helping Russia evade international sanctions.
* Blockchain analytics firm *Elliptic* said the move marks the first time the UK has used Regulation 17

realigned itself 
 with the European Union. But there are big obstacles, both financial and political, in the way of a closer relationship.

🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Markets enter cautious consolidation as Bitcoin volatility hits historic lows. While institutional accumulation from firms like Strive persists, heavy ETF outflows signal cooling retail demand. Regulatory clarity via the CLARITY Act remains the primary catalyst. 📉Bitcoin Volatility Hits 9-Month Low Amid $77K Consolidation Bitcoin’s expected volatility has dropped to its lowest level since 2023 as the asset struggles to break the $80,000 resistance. Market analysts point to $1 billion in monthly ETF outflows as a sign of temporary cooling in retail demand. Read full story 💰Strive Asset Management Adds 1,109 Bitcoin to Treasury Strive (ASST) has increased its total holdings to 16,500 BTC, positioning itself as the seventh-largest public corporate holder globally. The firm continues a strategy of aggressive accumulation to provide shareholders with direct digital asset exposure. See details 🏛️Digital Asset Market Clarity Act Advances Through Senate The CLARITY Act has successfully passed the Senate Banking Committee, moving closer to a final July 4 deadline. The legislation aims to unify US crypto oversight and provide the regulatory certainty needed for multi-trillion dollar institutional entries. Read full story 🚨White House Executive Order Mandates Regulatory Reassessment President Trump signed a directive ordering federal agencies to eliminate outdated barriers hindering digital asset integration. The order specifically targets the removal of restrictive accounting rules and encourages bank-grade crypto custody. See details 💎Bitmine Immersion Holdings Reach 4.4% of Total Ether Supply Bitmine (BMNR) now holds 5.39 million ETH, valued at over $10 billion, following a massive staking-focused accumulation drive. The firm's total crypto and cash reserves have reached a milestone of $12.3 billion. Read full story 🚀NEAR Protocol Surges 15% on AI-Native Blockchain Upgrades Defying a broader market dip, NEAR Protocol hit a six-month high driven by anticipation of the v2.13 network upgrade. Investors are increasingly positioning in NEAR due to its specialized architecture for high-performance AI operations. See details 📊Crypto Funds Record $1.47 Billion in Weekly Outflows Global digital asset funds experienced their third-largest outflow of 2026 as investors pulled capital from major Bitcoin pro

73630.subject: 📢Prihlasovanie do cafeterie opäť funguje
73630.body:
Cafeteria login is working again

📢Prihlasovanie do cafeterie opäť funguje

( https://www.edenred.sk/blog/docasne-obmedzenie-cafeterie-edenred-benefity-premium/ )

English version below

Vážený klient, užívateľ,

 
zasielanie SMS správ, ktoré sú potrebné pre prihlásenie do cafeterie (multifaktorové overovanie), bolo úspešne obnovené.

Systém je opäť funkčný a cafeteriu už môžete znovu využívať.

 

Ďakujeme Vám za trpezlivosť a ospravedlňujeme sa za spôsobené nepríjemnosti.

 

S pozdravom,
Tím Edenred

 

Dear client, dear user,

the sending of SMS messages required for logging into the cafeteria (multi-factor authentication) has been successfully restored.

The system is working again, and you can now use the cafeteria once more.

Thank you for your patience, and we apologize for any inconvenience caused.

 

Best regards,
The Edenred Team

 

Všetky práva vyhradené © Ticket Service

73629.subject: The Mines Reward Conviction
73629.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

On a quiet Memorial Day of trading, we look at two divergences worth sitting with this week. First, HYPE's ETF inflows are outpacing what BTC ETFs saw at the equivalent point in their launch cycle, even as spot BTC ETFs post their worst week since January. Second, we dive into ORE, a Solana-native asset riding the privacy bid, though the real story is structural buy flows.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

With US markets closed for Memorial Day, the tape was thin. But crypto never sleeps. A quiet but risk-on session saw BTC close +0.2% in the high-$77K range after a rough weekend, following Trump’s Sunday [pronouncement](https://www.npr.org/2026/05/23/g-s1-124145/trump-iran-deal-strait-of-hormuz) that Iran negotiations were “proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner.”

We’ve seen this movie before, and it hasn’t so far ended with any meaningful reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. But, who knows, maybe this time is different.

Spot BTC ETFs bled[ ~$1.26B last week](https://www.theblock.co/post/402420/spot-bitcoin-etfs-shed-1-26-billion-in-worst-week-since-late-january-as-ether-funds-see-10-day-outflow-streak), the steepest weekly drawdown since late January, with May 18 alone accounting for $648M of single-day redemptions. Spot ether ETFs are in an even longer slump — 10 consecutive days of outflows through Friday. This is real flow-side weakness that hasn't fully resolved.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/0a3b47c1-f396-40cb-acde-dff978a91ddb/image.png?t=1779790412)
Caption: 

Equity benchmarks on the chart are flat by definition (no print), so the cross-sector dispersion today is an unusually clean read on intra-crypto positioning.

Modular (+13.2%) topped the leaderboard by a wide margin — more than double the next-best sector — primarily driven by TIA, which was up roughly 14% on Monday. Worth flagging that the underlying fundamentals haven't changed; Celestia currently sports modest daily activity and generates only $70-90 of REV, so this looks more like narrative-driven rotation piggybacking on the AI craze than a re-rating on usage.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/d849930b-64f6-4f71-83f9-6eb5a8e74337/image.png?t=1779790412)
Caption: 

Meme (+7.1%), DePIN (+6.2%), Launchpad (+2.8%), L2s (+2.2%), and AI (+1.8%) rounded out the top of the table.

On the downside, Perps (-2.5%), DeF
